name: Al Haig
abstract: Alan Warren Haig (July 19, 1922 – November 16, 1982) was an American jazz pianist, best known as one of the pioneers of bebop.

concerts.label: Horizons in Jazz featuring Dizzy Gillespie, George Shearing, and Others
Midnight Performance: Jazz at the Philharmonic

works.t: Walk a Little Faster: April in Paris
Wake Up and Dream!: What Is This Thing Called Love?
Born to Dance: Easy to Love

concerts.label: Al Haig, Piano, and Jimmy Raney, Guitar
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Wormworth
about_quotes.evidence: I was a close friend of the late Al Haig and worked with him for a long time and became a really close personal friend.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Wormworth interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: I got a call from Al Haig who I had met when he was working on 52nd Street with Charlie Parker. And he said, “Billy I need somebody to sub for me... I got something to do and if you could cover the rehearsal and the opening night for me down at Birdland I would appreciate it.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: Al Haig was there. Also Clyde Hart at one time or another. So that was my first thing as far as leading a group.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: 'Licks Apollo' Lips of Plenty with me, Gene Ramsey, Al Haig, Denzel Best, and John Collins.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I said, “Yeah, I’ll scat with him... So I walked up on the stage, and I scatted on The Song is You... Tommy Potter, Max Roach, Al Haig – were burnin’.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: They had Al Haig on piano... So we hooked up again. Charlie Parker and the band... we would manage to get together in the after-hour spots
